metadata.source_authority: Council of Ministers, Federal Democratic Republic of Ethiopia
metadata.source_note: Council of Ministers Federal Income Tax Regulation No. 410/2017, the Federal Negarit Gazette 23rd Year No. 82, Addis Ababa 24 August 2017 (issue line, regulation number and enabling recital verified on the face: "issued by the Council of Ministers ... [under Article] 99 of the Federal Income Tax Proclamation No. 979/2016"). Faithful reproduction hosted in the Ethiopian Legal Brief (chilot) WordPress repository and byte-verified against the SOUTHMOD/ETMOD harness copy; text-embedded, extracted as a single block with no OCR. This is the Schedule A/B/C implementing regulation providing the deduction, allowance and presumptive-computation detail behind the Proclamation 979/2016 rate tables. Flag: served from a third-party faithful-reproduction host (chilot); the gazette issue number and date are present on the face.
metadata.title: Council of Ministers Federal Income Tax Regulation No. 410/2017 - issued under Article 99 of Proclamation 979/2016 (Federal Negarit Gazette No. 82, 24 August 2017)

metadata.source_authority: Ministry of Finance, Federal Democratic Republic of Ethiopia
metadata.source_note: Official Ministry of Finance Directive No. 1021/2024, "A Directive to Provide the Amount of Electricity and Water Consumption for Domestic Use Exempt from Value Added Tax", dated 5 September 2024 and served from mofed.gov.et; byte-verified against the SOUTHMOD/ETMOD harness copy. A 3-page text-embedded PDF, extracted as a single block with no OCR. Made under Article 1(m) of Annex 2 of the Value Added Tax Proclamation No. 1341/2024 ("of Annex 2 of the Value Added Tax Proclamation No. 1341/2024, the following Directive is issued"). Grounds the exemption thresholds, verbatim "a) Monthly electricity consumption up to 200" [kWh] and "b) Monthly water consumption up to 15 cubic" [meters].
metadata.title: Ministry of Finance Directive No. 1021/2024 - VAT exemption for domestic electricity (up to 200 kWh) and water (up to 15 cubic meters) consumption
